references.title: An Approach to Group Formation in Collaborative Learning Using Learning Paths in Learning Management Systems

extracted.Courses: 2 editions of a Discrete Mathematics course
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
The LMS logs were converted into a Graph via the Plugin LPGraph.

The graph allows teachers to vizualize whether a learner followed the pre-specified learning path or if the learner differed.
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: Expert Validation and Learning Gains from Activity 1 to Activity 2
extracted.Cluster Description:
The goal of M-Cluster is to create groups. This creation can follow multiple paradigms - heterogenous to homogenous groups.

3 uneven clusters. With cluster 2 having 70% of the sample.
extracted.Comments:
Authors create their own conceptual framework for group formation - TARGAVA.

Authors group the students using the k-means algorithm and attempt 3 distinct forms of distance metric: Euclidean, Manhattan or Cosine.

All suggested groups were validated by an expert professor to select the best course of action.

Learners were allowed to form a team with members of the same cluster and, from activity 1 to  there was an overall improvement in performance of the students.
extracted.Limitations:
- No interpretation of the clusters

Only explores k-means as an algorithm.

annotations.text: Automated approaches to group formation are useful in distance learning situations. The approach of the authors uses LMS to understand learning paths and using these learning paths to make recommendations of teams for collaborative learning. Groups are done using k-means with attributes derived from different learning paths. REsults show that there is promise for improvement of student performance by up to 75%.
